name: SushiSwap API Vocabulary
description: Vocabulary of terms used in SushiSwap Blade and Sushi APIs

terms.definition: The EVM-compatible blockchain network identifier (e.g., 1 for Ethereum mainnet)
The contract address of an ERC-20 token on the specified chain
A liquidity pool containing two assets used for token swaps
An estimated output amount and price impact for a given swap without on-chain execution
An on-chain token exchange transaction aggregating liquidity across multiple DEX protocols
The USD value of a token as determined by on-chain liquidity sources
The total value of assets available in a pool for trading
The RP4 smart contract that aggregates and routes swaps across multiple DEX protocols
The maximum acceptable difference between expected and actual trade price, expressed in basis points
The exact amount of the input token to be swapped
The estimated amount of the output token received from the swap
The contract address of the input token to be sold
The contract address of the output token to be purchased
A liquidity pool whose data is sourced directly from on-chain smart contracts
A liquidity pool whose data is sourced from off-chain RFQ (Request for Quote) providers
Request for Quote — an off-chain mechanism for obtaining swap prices from market makers
A compact 64-byte Ethereum signature format per EIP-2098, combining r, s, and v values
A token pair consisting of two asset addresses representing a trading pair in a pool
A token or digital asset held in or traded through a liquidity pool
The percentage change in token price caused by the size of a swap relative to pool liquidity
A Unix timestamp after which a swap transaction will revert if not executed
The wallet address that receives the output tokens from a swap
The wallet address initiating and funding the swap transaction
The encoded transaction data passed to the Route Processor contract to execute a swap
The gas cost estimate in wei for executing a swap transaction on the target chain
